text:
You are building an admin product catalog page for an e-commerce team.

The catalog is read-heavy and should feel fast for day-to-day browsing, so avoid re-querying product data on every request.

Admins can trigger a "Sync latest catalog" action from the page when upstream ERP/PIM data changes (pricing, inventory, availability). After submitting, they should be able to continue working immediately, even if the product list is briefly stale.

The expected behavior is that product data is refreshed in the background and becomes up to date shortly after, across any views that depend on the same catalog data.

Implement the sync trigger as a regular HTML form that uses an inline Server Action in the page.
Use the cache tag name "products" consistently for catalog caching and invalidation.

In practice, sync jobs can touch thousands of SKUs, so operators prioritize a responsive admin experience and eventual consistency across catalog views over forcing every request to block on freshly recomputed data.

I’m going to inspect the app structure to find the catalog page, its data-fetching path, and the existing caching/server-action patterns, then I’ll wire the sync form to invalidate the shared `products` cache tag without making the request wait on recomputation.

I found a very small app surface, which is good: the catalog page likely lives in `app/page.tsx` and there’s a single DB helper. Next I’m reading those files to see how the page currently fetches data and where to place the inline action cleanly.

I’ve confirmed this is a bare Next app, so I need the exact Next cache APIs rather than guessing. I’m checking the official docs for the current `cacheTag` / `revalidateTag` pattern and server action form syntax, then I’ll patch the page and data layer together.

The docs confirm the right fit: cache the catalog with `use cache` + `cacheTag('products')`, and invalidate from the inline server action with `revalidateTag('products', 'max')` so the sync request returns quickly and the next view refreshes in the background. I’m checking whether the repo already has Next config before patching.

I have enough context to edit now. I’m going to keep the change minimal: convert the catalog page into an async server component, cache the product read behind the shared `products` tag, and add a native `<form>` with an inline action that invalidates that tag after the sync trigger.

I’ve got the exact API shape. The implementation will cache the read path in `lib/db.js`, then make the page use that cached helper and a form-scoped server action that calls `revalidateTag('products', 'max')` so the catalog stays responsive instead of forcing a fresh recompute on every submit.

The core change is in place. I’m running a build now to catch any Next-specific issues, especially around the inline server action and the caching directives, because those are the only parts likely to fail in this small app.

text: The build failure is specific: `use cache` is gated behind `cacheComponents`. I’m enabling that flag in `next.config.ts` and then rerunning the build to verify the page/action path compiles cleanly.
The cache feature flag is enabled. I’m doing one final build pass to confirm the cached catalog and invalidation flow now pass the Next compiler, not just the TypeScript surface.

text: The only remaining issue is a prerender restriction from `new Date()` in the server component. I’m removing that timestamp so the page stays fully static/cached and the build can complete.
The page is now free of runtime-only values, which keeps the cache path compatible with prerendering. I’m rerunning the build one last time to verify the final state.

text:
Implemented the catalog sync flow with cached reads and background invalidation.

What changed:
- Cached the product fetch in [`lib/db.js`](/home/user/workspace/lib/db.js) with `use cache`, `cacheLife('hours')`, and the shared tag `products`.
- Replaced the placeholder home page in [`app/page.tsx`](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx) with an admin catalog view.
- Added a regular HTML `<form>` with an inline Server Action that calls `revalidateTag('products', 'max')`, so admins can submit sync and keep working immediately.
- Enabled `cacheComponents` in [`next.config.ts`](/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts) so the cache directives compile in this Next canary build.

Verification:
- `npm run build` passes.

Behavior:
- Catalog reads are cached and tagged consistently with `products`.
- Sync invalidates that tag without forcing the request to wait on recomputation.
- Other views using the same tagged catalog data will refresh on the next access.

If you want, I can also split the catalog data rendering into a reusable component so additional admin views can consume the same cached source with less duplication.
